---
title: "Lesson 17: Simple Graphs"
---

## Learning goals

Create simple line charts and bar charts using `matplotlib`.

## Main ideas

Install matplotlib if needed.

```bash
python -m pip install matplotlib
```

Create a line chart.

```python
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

scores = [5, 7, 6, 8, 10]

plt.plot(scores)
plt.title("Score Progress")
plt.xlabel("Attempt")
plt.ylabel("Score")
plt.show()
```

Create a bar chart.

```python
names = ["Alex", "Ben", "Chloe"]
scores = [8, 10, 7]

plt.bar(names, scores)
plt.show()
```

## Exercises

### Exercise 1

Plot a list of scores as a line chart.

### Exercise 2

Add a title and axis labels.

### Exercise 3

Create a bar chart of three names and scores.

### Exercise 4

Plot practice minutes over five days.

## Debugging practice

Fix this deliberately broken code, then explain what was wrong.

```python
# Broken on purpose: read the error, fix one thing, and run again.
if True
print("debugging practice")
```

## Challenge

Build a small program that uses this lesson's idea together with at least two earlier ideas from the course.

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## Click to reveal hints

- Start with the smallest working version.
- Add one feature at a time.
- Use `print()` to inspect values.
- Test with more than one example.

:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Click to reveal one possible solution or approach

```python
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

names = ["Alex", "Ben", "Chloe"]
scores = [8, 10, 7]

plt.bar(names, scores)
plt.title("Quiz Scores")
plt.xlabel("Name")
plt.ylabel("Score")
plt.show()
```

:::

## What you learned

You can now create simple charts to show scores, progress and comparisons.

## Looking ahead

Next you will learn what packages are and how to install them safely.
